prompt_text:
Analyze the following python source code for violations of the Liskov Substitution Principle (LSP).

Use a TWO-STAGE reasoning process:

## Stage 1 — List design smells (silent, for your own reasoning)
Identify relevant low-level design smells you observe in the code:
- God Class, Feature Envy, Shotgun Surgery, Divergent Change
- Long Method, Large Class, Long Parameter List
- Refused Bequest, Inappropriate Intimacy, Message Chains
- Switch Statements on type, Data Clumps
Think through these int...
raw_response:
{
  "findings": [
    {
      "file_path": "requests/src/requests/structures.py",
      "entity_name": "LookupDict.__getitem__",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 79,
      "line_end": 82,
      "principle": "LSP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "Subclass changes the expected behavior of the parent dict's access method.",
      "reasoning": "Divergent Change smell -> LSP violation because LookupDict inherits from 'dict' but overrides __getitem__ to return None for missing keys instead of raising a KeyError. This breaks the fundamental contract of the Python dictionary interface, where consumers expect a KeyError when accessing a non-existent key via the bracket operator []."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "requests/src/requests/auth.py",
      "entity_name": "HTTPProxyAuth.__call__",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 107,
      "line_end": 109,
      "principle": "LSP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Subclass overrides behavior in a way that breaks the base class contract.",
      "reasoning": "Refused Bequest smell -> LSP violation because HTTPProxyAuth inherits from HTTPBasicAuth but changes the side effect of the __call__ method. While HTTPBasicAuth is contracted to set the 'Authorization' header, HTTPProxyAuth sets the 'Proxy-Authorization' header instead. A component expecting an HTTPBasicAuth object to authorize a request will find the 'Authorization' header missing if passed an HTTPProxyAuth instance."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "requests/src/requests/cookies.py",
      "entity_name": "MockRequest.add_header",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 77,
      "line_end": 81,
      "principle": "LSP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Subclass nullifies a parent class method by raising NotImplementedError.",
      "reasoning": "Refused Bequest smell -> LSP violation because MockRequest explicitly mimics the urllib2.Request interface but raises NotImplementedError for the add_header method. This violates the substitution principle where a subtype (the mock) should be able to stand in for the base type (the real request) without throwing unexpected errors for standard interface methods."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "requests/src/requests/cookies.py",
      "entity_name": "RequestsCookieJar",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 201,
      "line_end": 383,
      "principle": "LSP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "Subclass violates postconditions and exception contracts of the Mapping interface.",
      "reasoning": "Behavioral Change smell -> LSP violation because RequestsCookieJar implements the MutableMapping interface but breaks its contract in two ways: 1) __getitem__ (via _find_no_duplicates) raises a custom CookieConflictError (RuntimeError) instead of a KeyError or returning a value, which is unexpected for standard mapping consumers. 2) __setitem__ (via set) deletes a key if the value is None, whereas the Mapping contract requires that setting a key to None should result in the key existing with a None value."
    }
  ]
}

findings.description: Subclass changes the expected behavior of the parent dict's access method.
findings.reasoning: Divergent Change smell -> LSP violation because LookupDict inherits from 'dict' but overrides __getitem__ to return None for missing keys instead of raising a KeyError. This breaks the fundamental contract of the Python dictionary interface, where consumers expect a KeyError when accessing a non-existent key via the bracket operator [].

findings.description: Subclass overrides behavior in a way that breaks the base class contract.
findings.reasoning: Refused Bequest smell -> LSP violation because HTTPProxyAuth inherits from HTTPBasicAuth but changes the side effect of the __call__ method. While HTTPBasicAuth is contracted to set the 'Authorization' header, HTTPProxyAuth sets the 'Proxy-Authorization' header instead. A component expecting an HTTPBasicAuth object to authorize a request will find the 'Authorization' header missing if passed an HTTPProxyAuth instance.

findings.description: Subclass nullifies a parent class method by raising NotImplementedError.
findings.reasoning: Refused Bequest smell -> LSP violation because MockRequest explicitly mimics the urllib2.Request interface but raises NotImplementedError for the add_header method. This violates the substitution principle where a subtype (the mock) should be able to stand in for the base type (the real request) without throwing unexpected errors for standard interface methods.

findings.description: Subclass violates postconditions and exception contracts of the Mapping interface.
findings.reasoning: Behavioral Change smell -> LSP violation because RequestsCookieJar implements the MutableMapping interface but breaks its contract in two ways: 1) __getitem__ (via _find_no_duplicates) raises a custom CookieConflictError (RuntimeError) instead of a KeyError or returning a value, which is unexpected for standard mapping consumers. 2) __setitem__ (via set) deletes a key if the value is None, whereas the Mapping contract requires that setting a key to None should result in the key existing with a None value.
